prompt: Name a song you find genuinely moving — any language, any era — and describe, in a short paragraph, why it works on you.
response: One song I find genuinely moving is *“Ne me quitte pas”* by Jacques Brel. In this 1959 French chanson, Brel pleads with a lover not to leave, weaving desperation, vulnerability, and poetic imagery into every line. What makes it so powerful is the raw emotional intensity—his voice trembles with both pride and supplication, offering the world and reducing himself to a servant if only love is spared. The lyrics are simultaneously grand and intimate, transforming personal heartbreak into something universal. It works on me because it doesn’t shy from the ache of loss, yet finds beauty in the surrender to love, even in its most fragile, pleading form.
